---
title: "Week 4 — Hands-on bioinformatics II & capstone"
subtitle: "Literature, protocols, and your scoped capstone"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Scope reminder
The capstone was *scoped in week 3*. Week 4 is execution and writeup, not start-to-finish. An honest 4-week course cannot deliver an "end-to-end research project" cold.
:::

## Topics

- AI-assisted literature review, with verification.
- AI as a sparring partner for protocol design.
- Capstone execution and presentations.

## Before class

Read:

- [Literature review](../bioinformatics/literature-review.qmd)
- [Protocol design](../bioinformatics/protocol-design.qmd)

Setup:

- Capstone proposal (from week 3) finalized after instructor feedback.

## In class

- Demo: triaging a new subfield with a grounded literature tool, verifying every citation against PubMed/CrossRef.
- Demo: iterating on a protocol with an AI sparring partner — generate, critique, refine.
- Capstone working session and peer feedback.
- Lightning presentations.

## After class

- **Capstone** *(30%)*: deliver on the scoped proposal from week 3. Submit:
  1. **Literature brief**: ≤ 5 verified citations, each with a one-sentence relevance note. Verification means each citation has been checked on PubMed or CrossRef.
  2. **Analysis or protocol** (one-page): a proposed analysis (with code in a Quarto/Jupyter report) *or* a proposed protocol (with controls, sample size reasoning, expected outcomes).
  3. **4 D's reflection** (≤ 500 words): map your process onto Delegation, Description, Discernment, Diligence. Where did each show up? Where did you fail and recover?
  4. **Disclosure statement** per the [rubric](../course-syllabus.qmd#disclosure-rubric).

## Wrap-up

- What worked, what didn't.
- Commitments: what habits will you keep after the course?
- Honest acknowledgment: this is a *foundations* course. Real fluency develops over months of supervised practice. Plan accordingly.

## Supplementary

Slides, recording, and notebooks will be posted here after each session.
